title: Vaccinium melliflorum
intro: Vaccinium melliflorum, also known as the Mayflower blueberry or blue mayflower, is a small, shrub-like plant native to the eastern United States, belonging to the heath family (Ericaceae). The plant is rich in health benefits, including its potential to aid in reducing inflammation, improve cardiovascular health, and exhibit antioxidant properties. Bioactive constituents of Vaccinium melliflorum include anthocyanins, ellagic acid, and phenolic acids, which contribute to its medicinal properties. The plant is commonly used to make herbal preparations such as teas, tinctures, and infusions, which are often consumed to support immune function and overall well-being.
